html:
Exclusive to:  

The Trawl Net is a Boat attachment from the Shipwrecked DLC. It is crafted from 3 Rope and 2 Bamboo and must be prototyped at the Science Machine. It is also a reward from the Yaarctopus in exchange for Bisque. Trawl Nets cannot be attached to rafts.

The Trawl Net is used for capturing small marine life and items on the water bed. It has 9 storage slots and drops upon being full. It can be detached manually before it is full, but it cannot be attached or retrieved again. The player can click on a detached Trawl Net to open it and retrieve the contents. Detached Trawl Nets cannot be retrieved, and will sink 90 seconds after being detached, dropping any items it contains once it disappears. If the player fails to collect them before the Trawl Net sinks, any living creatures will die and their dead versions will drop instead. Items caught in the Trawl Net will not stack even if they normally would in the inventory.

The Trawl Net is equipped in the same slot on a boat as Sails, forcing the player to paddle the boat. A new item can be caught in the Trawl Net for every 100 units traveled in any direction. Each item caught reduces the player's movement speed by about 2.8%, up to a maximum penalty of about -25% with 9 items caught. Dragging the Trawl Net over a Mussel spawn will yield a single mussel and consume the spawn. Jellyfish and Wobsters can also be caught with the Trawl Net by sailing close by them and catching them in the net. Trawl Nets are the only way to acquire Seaweed Stalks and Sunken Prefabs. Bioluminescence can also be collected by the Trawl Net during the Dusk and the night time. The Quacken has a chance to spawn every time an item is collected in the deep ocean.

Additionally, items that sunk in the Ocean can be retrieved by sailing over them with an attached Trawl Net. These items will periodically generate bubbles to indicate their location. Items can only be retrieved this way if they didn't sink in the Deep Ocean, and less than 2 days have passed since they sunk. Items that remain sunk in the ocean after 2 days, are lost forever, and cannot be retrieved. Items sunk in Deep Ocean tiles are immediately lost and cannot be retrieved.

Loot

The following items can be obtained through the Trawl Net depending on the Season:

Mild and Monsoon Season

Ocean Layer Chance Acquirable Items
Shallow 19.1%
9.5%
4.8%
Medium 11.0%
5.5%
2.7%
1.4%
Deep 7.8%  
3.9%
1.9%  
1.0%

Hurricane Season

Ocean Layer Chance Acquirable Items
Shallow 12.1%
6.1%
3.0%
Medium 7.8%
3.9%
2.0%
1.0%
Deep 5.6%
2.8%
1.4%
0.7%

Dry Season

Ocean Layer Chance Acquirable Items
Shallow 11.4%
5.7%
2.9%
Medium 7.7%
3.8%
1.9%
1.0%
Deep 5.7%
2.9%
1.4%
0.7%

Hamlet

The Trawl Net blueprint needs to be purchased from the The Tinkerer's Tower for 30 Oincs, as the player needs to learn the recipe before its available for crafting.

All Seasons

Lilly Ponds
Item Chance Individual Chance
47.05% 7.84%
27.45% 3.92%
15.68% 1.96%
9.8% 0.98%

Tips

  • When collecting Seaweed Stalks, the Seaweed itself should be harvested before sailing over it with the Trawl Net in order to save space. Otherwise, a Seaweed and a Seaweed Stalk will be collected and take up 2 slots in the net.
  • It is recommended to use faster boats when trawling for items. While the Trawl Net is mutually exclusive with Sail, you can still increase the boat's speed with items such as Walking Cane, Sleek Hat as well as speed boosting consumables such as Coffee, Purple Groupers, Tropical Bouillabaisse, etc.
  • If summoning the Quacken quickly is the intent, players can drop common items such as twigs and grass in front of their boat then sail over them in the deep ocean to speed up the process.
  • To get Pan Flutes or the Trident, trawling in deep water during Hurricane Season or Dry Season is optimal, although there isn't too much difference in chances of getting them during Mild and Monsoon season.
  • To get Gold Nuggets or Dubloons, trawling in medium water during Hurricane Season or deep water during Mild and Monsoon seasons is the best, with medium water in Dry Season close behind.
  • To get Obsidian, trawling in shallow water during Dry Season is the best way to obtain it through the Trawl Net.

Trivia

  • The intended maximum speed penalty when using the trawl net is likely 25%, but because of the way the speed penalty is programmed, it only reaches about 22.2%, eight ninths of 25%.

Gallery

  • A Cargo Boat with a Trawl Net attached.
  • Collecting with a Trawl Net.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

"trawlnet"
“Nothing but net.”
–Wilson
“I'll catch a bunch of wet stuff with this.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang will trap you in his net!”
–Wolfgang
“A way to wrest items from the sea's grip.”
–Wendy
“USE IN BAD WEATHER FOR BEST RESULTS”
–WX-78
“For collecting debris at sea.”
–Wickerbottom
“The fishes should swim right into this.”
–Woodie
“I see no downside to using this.”
–Maxwell
“What enigmas of the deep can I uncover!”
–Wagstaff
“I shall ransack the sea!”
–Wigfrid
“I wonder what we'll drag up.”
–Webber
“Lootin' and cruisin'.”
–Walani
“Ah! The life of a fisherman!”
–Warly
“Fer trawl'n around.”
–Woodlegs
“WHEREIN I'LL CATCH THE FISHIES OF THE SEA”
–Wilba
“Glub Glub Snagger”
–Wormwood
“A lot drier than diving for treasure.”
–Wheeler

It is crafted from 3 [[Rope]] and 2 [[Bamboo]] and must be prototyped at the [[Science Machine]]. It is also a reward from the [[Yaarctopus]] in exchange for [[Bisque]]. Trawl Nets cannot be attached to rafts. The Trawl Net is used for capturing small marine life and items on the water bed. It has 9 storage slots and drops upon being full. It can be detached manually before it is full, but it cannot be attached or retrieved again. The player can click on a detached Trawl Net to open it and retrieve the contents. Detached Trawl Nets cannot be retrieved, and will sink 90 seconds after being detached, dropping any items it contains once it disappears. If the player fails to collect them before the Trawl Net sinks, any living creatures will die and their dead versions will drop instead. Items caught in the Trawl Net will not stack even if they normally would in the inventory. The Trawl Net is equipped in the same slot on a boat as [[Sails]], forcing the player to paddle the boat. A new item can be caught in the Trawl Net for every 100 units traveled in any direction. Each item caught reduces the player's movement speed by about 2.8%, up to a maximum penalty of about -25% with 9 items caught. Dragging the Trawl Net over a [[Mussel]] spawn will yield a single mussel and consume the spawn. [[Jellyfish]] and [[Wobster]]s can also be caught with the Trawl Net by sailing close by them and catching them in the net. Trawl Nets are the only way to acquire [[Seaweed Plant|Seaweed Stalks]] and Sunken Prefabs. [[Bioluminescence]] can also be collected by the Trawl Net during the [[Dusk]] and the night time. The [[Quacken]] has a chance to spawn every time an item is collected in the deep ocean. Additionally, items that sunk in the Ocean can be retrieved by sailing over them with an attached Trawl Net. These items will periodically generate bubbles to indicate their location. Items can only be retrieved this way if they didn't sink in the [[Ocean#Shipwrecked|Deep Ocean]], and less than 2 days have passed since they sunk. Items that remain sunk in the ocean after 2 days, are lost forever, and cannot be retrieved. Items sunk in Deep Ocean tiles are immediately lost and cannot be retrieved. __NOTOC__ == {{Pic32|Dubloons}} Loot== The following items can be obtained through the Trawl Net depending on the [[Seasons#Shipwrecked|Season]]: ===Mild and Monsoon Season=== {| class="wikitable" border="0" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 100%;" |- !
